ocr.text: In Q1 2024, the airline load factor trends reveal valuable insights across several major carriers in North America, with Air
Canada leading at a load factor of 82.5%, passenger numbers reflecting a 12.3% increase, and revenue reaching $1,500 million.
Following closely is WestJet, which recorded a load factor of 79.0%, an 8.5% rise in passenger numbers, and a revenue total of
$1,200 million. Delta Airlines has a load factor of 76.5%, with passenger numbers showing a 10.0% increase and revenue at
$900 million, while United Airlines reported a load factor of 78.2%, an increase of 7.8% in passenger numbers, and revenue
totaling $850 million. Southwest Airlines achieved a load factor of 80.5%, with passenger numbers up by 9.2% and revenue
The overall total for the airline sector indicates a load factor of 79.8%, a 10.3% increase in passenger numbers, and combined
revenue of $4,400 million, representing a year-over-year change of 2.5% in load factor, with not applicable (N/A) entries for
passenger numbers and revenue under this category. In terms of market share, Air Canada holds a significant 30.0%, followed
by WestJet at 25.0%, Delta at 15.0%, United at 10.0%, and Southwest at 20.0%, resulting in a total market share of 100.0%.
These figures illustrate a competitive landscape among the airlines, highlighting the growth dynamics in passenger volume and
revenue generation, which are critical for strategic planning and operational efficiency moving forward.
